key_themes: Biofuel policy uncertainty and compressed crush margins
Q4 2025 EPS beat but sharp revenue miss and disappointing 2026 guidance below consensus
Dividend growth and cost-saving plans amid mixed analyst Hold ratings and varied price targets
sources_checked: Reuters, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat, Yahoo Finance analyst coverage
Recent X posts on $ADM and biofuels (Jan-Apr 2026)
Stock price history and performance data through mid-April 2026
summary: ADM beat Q4 2025 adjusted EPS ($0.87 vs ~$0.80-0.81 expected) but revenue fell sharply (~14% YoY to $18.56B, missing estimates), with full-year 2025 adjusted EPS at $3.43 after an 81% collapse in crushing profits due to weak soybean oil demand and deferred U.S. biofuel policy. The 2026 EPS guide of $3.60-4.25 has a midpoint below consensus (~$4.24), with the low end assuming flat margins and continued policy delays; shares tumbled post-release though some recovery has occurred amid dividend raise (now 94+ years) and cost initiatives. Biggest risk is prolonged U.S. biofuel/RVO uncertainty preventing crush margin recovery in the core Ag Services & Oilseeds segment.[[1]](https://investors.adm.com/news/news-details/2026/ADM-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-year-2025-Results-Provides-2026-Guidance/default.aspx)[[2]](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/adm-shares-tumble-as-2026-guidance-disappoints-despite-q4-earnings-beat-93CH-4481235)[[3]](https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-energy/grain-trader-adm-forecasts-downbeat-2026-profit-us-biofuel-policy-delay-2026-02-03/)

recent_posts.highlight: Nice uptrend on $ADM. Solid stock and dividend payer trading above the 21 EMA.
Record March soybean crush and Vietnam E10/biofuel rollout noted as potential tailwinds for $ADM and $BG.
$ADM listed among consumer staples or 'stocks on sale' in value/income discussions alongside dividend peers.
Biofuel policy and inventory gains from ag commodities/ Iran-related moves seen as eventual upside for $ADM.
